covered_earnings_window.plain_language: Weekend run as of Saturday, June 13, 2026 at 12:03 p.m. EDT: confirmed U.S.-listed companies reporting before the next U.S. market open on Monday, June 15, 2026. No still-actionable after-close session remains before that open; Friday, June 12 after-close results were already public.

market_context_summary: U.S. indexes finished higher on Friday, June 12, with small caps leading weekly gains as oil fell on U.S.-Iran peace optimism and risk appetite improved. The thin Monday before-open earnings window is small-cap-heavy, so liquidity, bid-ask spreads, and macro risk can dominate company-specific earnings reads.

excluded_companies.reason: After-close/weak timing confirmation and late filing context
Already reported June 11; not a normal pending operating-company earnings event

ranked_table.earnings_timing: Jun. 15, 2026 before open; call 8:30 a.m. ET
ranked_table.expected_driver: Services revenue, adjusted EBITDA, FY27 tone
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: Powerfleet has the cleanest setup in this thin window because recurring services growth, adjusted EBITDA expansion, and deleveraging give the market concrete metrics to reward if management validates the FY27 ramp.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: Consensus-type sources point to roughly breakeven to low-positive EPS and about $113M revenue; Q3 revenue was $113.5M and adjusted EBITDA was $25.7M.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Services revenue stays near double-digit growth, adjusted EBITDA margin holds, and management validates deleveraging plus large-contract/subscriber ramp timing.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: A flat revenue quarter, delayed subscriber ramp, leadership-transition questions, or higher investment spending could trigger disappointment.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: MarketChameleon showed a prior high-teens implied move for AIOT earnings; MarketBeat short-interest data showed 10.48% of float short with 7.3 days to cover as of May 29, 2026.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Limited detailed revision data; public estimate sources cluster around breakeven to low-positive EPS and roughly $113M revenue.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: Jun. 15, 2026 before open; call 10:00 a.m. ET
ranked_table.method_update_note: Allocation caution from repeated guide-risk lesson
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: CODA leans up because Q1 showed broad revenue growth and strong Marine Technology momentum, but the stock is still small and order timing can overwhelm a headline beat.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: Q1 revenue rose 28.8% to about $6.7M; third-party estimates for Q2 range from roughly $7.4M to $7.5M revenue and $0.12-$0.13 EPS.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Marine Technology growth holds, defense engineering contributes, and gross margin remains near the mid-60% range.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Any order-timing pause, margin mix shift, or cautious defense procurement commentary could reverse the small-cap momentum.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: Meaningful listed-options implied-move data was not available; thin liquidity is a larger practical risk than options pricing.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Estimate sources are sparse but constructive; one source showed Q2 EPS around $0.13 and revenue around $7.52M.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: Jun. 15, 2026 before open; webcast 10:00 a.m. ET
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: The base case leans down because the market is likely to focus on whether adjusted EBITDA progress and cash burn can offset restatement noise, margin pressure, and a still-lossmaking cannabis backdrop.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: The company will also file restated FY2024/FY2025 results tied to a non-cash warrant classification error; prior Q3 net revenue was flat at $75M, adjusted EBITDA loss narrowed to $3M, and free cash outflow improved to $19M.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Cost reductions, cash balance, Canadian adult-use progress, Storz & Bickel recovery, or a clearly less-bad FY2027 outlook could spark relief.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Restatement optics, weak gross margin, cash burn, international softness, or an unconvincing path to positive adjusted EBITDA could dominate.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: OptionSlam showed a roughly 12% weekly implied move and 5.7 short-interest metric; MarketBeat showed 5.61% of float short as of May 29.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Public earnings pages point to an expected EPS loss around $0.06, but consensus revenue figures differ by source/currency treatment, so revenue surprise quality is less clean.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: Jun. 15, 2026 before open; call 9:00 a.m. ET
ranked_table.expected_driver: Q4 revenue growth and gross margin
ranked_table.method_update_note: Allocation caution from repeated guide-risk lesson
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: Jerash has a modest upside setup if Q4 confirms guided revenue growth and acceptable gross margin, but thin trading and customer concentration keep conviction low.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: The company guided Q4 revenue growth of 23%-26% and gross margin around 14%-16%; MarketBeat showed expected revenue of $36.20M and EPS of -$0.01, while other third-party previews differ.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Revenue comes in near the guided range, margin holds, and management sounds confident on capacity expansion and customer diversification.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Any miss on revenue/gross margin, geopolitical/logistics pressure, or weak order commentary from major apparel customers could erase the edge.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: No meaningful options data found; StockTitan flagged low volume around the announcement and MarketBeat showed low short interest.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Coverage is thin and estimates conflict, reducing evidence quality.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: Jun. 15, 2026 before open; call 8:30 a.m. ET
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: Comtech leans down because the market may demand revenue conversion and balance-sheet progress after a prior revenue miss, even though bookings, backlog, and adjusted EBITDA improved.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: Q2 net sales were $106.8M versus revenue expectations around $113.85M, while adjusted EBITDA improved and backlog stood at $731.6M; MarketBeat listed Q3 consensus EPS around -$0.27 and revenue around $110.21M.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Book-to-bill remains strong, backlog visibility improves, and adjusted EBITDA/cash flow validates the turnaround.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Another revenue shortfall, weak backlog conversion, preferred/debt overhang, or cautious liquidity commentary could pressure shares.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: MarketChameleon showed CMTL's prior earnings implied move was very large, around 25%; that makes direction hard to separate from volatility.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Consensus remains loss-making and revenue recovery is still the central debate.

qualified_names.why: Absolute score above 30, reversal risk below 40, medium confidence, low disparity, best evidence quality
Absolute score above 30 and reversal risk below 40, but small-cap/liquidity and guide-risk caution limit weight
Thin window; remaining names fail score, confidence, or reversal-risk filters

excluded_high_reversal_or_weak_names.reason: Absolute score qualifies, but reversal risk is far above 40
Absolute score below 30 and reversal risk above 40
Absolute score below 30 and reversal risk above 40

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice.

consensus_method_note.disparity_definition: 0-100 measure of how far apart the three independent passes were across direction and reversal-risk judgments; lower means tighter agreement.
consensus_method_note.strongest_names_disparity: AIOT, CODA, CGC, and JRSH all had low numerical disparity. CGC still carried low confidence because reversal risk was high. CMTL had low-to-moderate disparity with one neutral pass.
consensus_method_note.effect_on_confidence_and_allocation: Low disparity helped AIOT and CODA, but allocation required reversal risk at or below 40 and an absolute score above 30. High reversal risk excluded CGC despite a stronger bearish score.
consensus_method_note.method_change_from_lessons: No new method change; existing strict timing, forward-look, key-metric-first, and research-allocation reversal-risk guardrails were applied.

disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
